excerpt: Oxlint is built for large repositories and CI environments. Benchmarks show Oxlint is 50 to 100 times faster than ESLint. Recommended migration: run Oxlint first, then ESLint with overlapping rules disabled. Install with pnpm add -D oxlint; add scripts lint and lint:fix.

Modernize your toolchain — Bun, Rspack, and oxlint are drop-in replacements that delivered 30-40% speedups per job with minimal migration effort. Turbo runs oxlint task across all packages as a fast pre-check before full ESLint.

Turborepo speeds up builds, lints, tests through parallelization and Remote Caching. Use turbo query affected to skip work when a cache hit is inevitable. Pass TURBO_TOKEN and TURBO_TEAM in CI for remote cache hits.

Turborepo 3.0 ships first-class git worktree support so multiple AI coding agents can work in parallel without cache thrash. git worktree add ../agent-1 feat/voice-prompt; turbo run build shares cache across worktrees. Cold cache full build ~120s, warm ~12s.

AgentAssay is the first token-efficient framework for regression testing non-deterministic AI agent workflows, achieving 78-100% cost reduction at equivalent statistical guarantees, with 86% behavioral detection power where binary testing has 0%. Defines mutation operators for prompts, tools, models, and context windows.

L4 workflow: agent generates code and tests, mutation testing runs against generated tests, surviving mutants flagged for regeneration. Set 70-80% kill rate threshold. Use incremental mutation testing in agent sandbox — Stryker --since only mutates changed code; 200-line PR takes 3-5 minutes not 40.

Configure OpenTelemetry to track requests end-to-end across application workloads, MCP servers, and Llama Stack. Use W3C Trace Context propagation. Auto-instrument HTTPx and FastAPI; manually integrate spans for agent reasoning steps.

Wrap each agent run in a root span; nest child spans for LLM calls, tool calls, retrieval. Use gen_ai.* semantic conventions. Sample by error and latency, not at random. Export via OTLP to any backend.

Vercel Sandbox runs untrusted code in Firecracker microVMs with node24/python3.13 runtimes. Use @vercel/sandbox SDK: Sandbox.create, runCommand. Sandboxes start in milliseconds; snapshotting skips dependency install on subsequent runs.

Sandbox is generally available as the execution layer for agents. Sub-second starts for thousands of sandboxes; pay only for active CPU time not idle. Blackbox AI dispatches tasks to multiple agents in parallel, each in an isolated sandbox.

Use AGENTS.md for simple cross-tool instructions; use .cursor/rules/*.mdc for glob-scoped rules with alwaysApply, description, globs. Keep rules under 500 lines. Nested AGENTS.md in subdirectories applies when working in that tree. Team Rules take precedence over Project Rules.

Cache-Warm Sequencing (CWS) treats Claude 5-minute prompt-cache TTL as a scheduling constraint. Batch independent subagents into parallel warm windows; naive sequential five subagents spans three cache windows, CWS batch fires all five at T=0 sharing one cache write. Target 80%+ hit rate on shared prefix.

Prompt caching reduces costs for repeated prefix content. cache_control marks stable blocks. Cache reads billed at reduced rate; monitor cache_read_input_tokens. Use prompt_cache_key for routing stickiness across inference backends.
